content.full_text:
FPPC

April 30, 1991
MAY 2 8 25 AM '91

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
FPPC
PO Box 807
Sacramento, Ca 95812

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

It is my understanding that if a locally elected official receives a gift with a value in
excess of $1000 the recipient must report the gift on the annual report and reimburse
the donor for the amount that exceeds $1000 within 30 days of the receipt of the gift.

I would appreciate a response to the following questions:

May the recipient base the value reported on a verbal estimate by the donor or, absent
information from the donor, on a good faith estimate by the recipient?

Is the donor required to maintain any documentation?

Does the law require that the reimbursement be in the form of money?

Can the donor be reimbursed with a comparable gift or with services of equivalent
value?

What does the FPPC consider adequate documentation of the reimbursement?


Very truly yours,

James J. Del Bon
674 Peach Street
Novato, Ca 94945

sections.question: May the recipient base the value reported on a verbal estimate by the donor or, absent information from the donor, on a good faith estimate by the recipient? Is the donor required to maintain any documentation? Does the law require that the reimbursement be in the form of money? Can the donor be reimbursed with a comparable gift or with services of equivalent value? What does the FPPC consider adequate documentation of the reimbursement?
sections.question_synthetic: A locally elected official seeks clarification on the procedures for reporting and reimbursing gifts exceeding $1000, including valuation methods, documentation requirements, and acceptable forms of reimbursement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be an incoming request letter rather than an FPPC response/advice letter. No conclusion or answer section is present. The document header is unclear and may contain OCR errors. Address information is incomplete at the bottom of the document.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from an individual (possibly Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel at FPPC based on the addressee line confusion in the document) asking five specific questions about gift reporting and reimbursement requirements for locally elected officials under California law.
